title: aquilegia pyrenaica subsp. bertolonii
intro: Aquilegia pyrenaica subsp. bertolonii, also known as the Pyrenean Columbine, is a rare and endangered plant species that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and promoting digestion, and has been used to treat conditions like rheumatism and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Aquilegia pyrenaica subsp. bertolonii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
